description: The repository root holds source entrypoints, maintenance docs, CSV audits, logs, MAT data, and transient probes together instead of acting as a controlled index layer.
evidence: Root has 139 files including 57 run_* files, 14 markdown files, 12 csv files, 11 log files, and 73 m-files.
impact: High ambiguity about what is authoritative, durable, transient, or safe to touch.
recommended_resolution: Limit root to repo metadata and minimal bootstrap files; route new scripts to scripts/ or module source, durable docs to docs/ or reports/, tables to tables/, and transient outputs to results/<module>/runs/.

affected_paths: Switching; Aging; Relaxation ver3; MT ver2
description: The active modules use different internal conventions for the same roles: Aging is deeply structured, Switching is shallow, Relaxation is mostly flat, and MT is fully flat.
evidence: Aging contains analysis/ diagnostics/ models/ pipeline/ plots/ tests/ utils/ docs/ verification; Switching contains analysis/ utils; Relaxation ver3 is mostly root-level plus diagnostics; MT ver2 has no subdirectories.
impact: Cross-module onboarding and automation must special-case every module.
recommended_resolution: Document a minimal allowed module convention and gradually align new files to it without forcing immediate renames of historical trees.

description: Source and artifact layers use different names for the same module.
evidence: The source folder is MT ver2 while the run-output tree is results/mt.
impact: Automations and humans must remember alias translations, increasing routing mistakes.
recommended_resolution: Publish an explicit module alias table now, and defer any physical rename until code-reference checks are complete.

description: Figure exports are split between results/ subtrees and the top-level figures/ namespace.
evidence: results/aging/figures contains png/fig/csv/md while figures/aging is empty and figures/relaxation is populated independently.
impact: It is unclear which figure location is canonical for long-term reference.
recommended_resolution: Declare figures/ as the durable export layer and keep results/*/figures only for run-scoped ephemeral products unless lineage rules explicitly promote them.

description: The durable reports area also contains code, scripts, logs, and CSVs.
evidence: reports contains md plus m, ps1, csv, json, partial_do_not_use, and log files.
impact: Durable reporting and execution support are blended, which weakens report lineage.
recommended_resolution: Reserve reports/ for durable narrative outputs only; place supporting execution code in scripts/ or tools/ and keep maintenance run logs under results or maintenance run folders.

description: The durable tables layer contains source-like files and ad hoc sidecar text/json content.
evidence: tables/aging contains hundreds of csv files but also an m-file, json exports, and txt sidecars.
impact: It becomes difficult to distinguish canonical tabular outputs from helper logic or staging metadata.
recommended_resolution: Reserve tables/ for durable structured exports and move source helpers elsewhere; keep any required sidecar manifests under a documented pattern.

description: Shared analysis code also stores logs and knowledge CSVs in the same namespace.
evidence: analysis contains m, ps1, txt, and log files; analysis/knowledge contains csv alongside code.
impact: Shared code becomes a dumping ground for both logic and generated evidence.
recommended_resolution: Keep reusable analysis code in analysis/ or tools/, and route knowledge tables or generated evidence into tables/analysis or docs/analysis by policy.

description: Reusable infrastructure and disposable execution wrappers are split across both tools/ and scripts/ without a sharp contract.
evidence: Both folders contain mixtures of m, ps1, and py files; scripts also contains csv, md, txt, and log artifacts.
impact: New helpers can land in either place, causing drift and duplication.
recommended_resolution: Define tools/ for reusable infrastructure and scripts/ for stable top-level orchestrators only; route disposable outputs elsewhere.

affected_paths: results; runs; logs; tmp; probe_outputs; status; root
description: Transient execution evidence is scattered across multiple unrelated top-level namespaces.
evidence: Run logs and probe outputs appear under results/*/runs, runs/, logs/, tmp/, status/, and root-level *.log/*.txt files.
impact: Operators cannot tell where fresh runtime evidence should live or where it can be safely cleaned up.
recommended_resolution: Adopt results/<module>/runs/<run_id>/ as the standard run container and reserve a maintenance run area for repo-health executions.

affected_paths: Aging old; results_old; tables_old; archive; _legacy; tmp_root_cleanup_quarantine; junk
description: Legacy, archival, stale, and quarantine material is spread across several differently named holding areas.
evidence: The repo uses old, results_old, tables_old, archive, _legacy, quarantine, and junk names for similar governance states.
impact: It is unclear which areas are write-closed, recoverable, experimental, or still active.
recommended_resolution: Publish a single legacy/quarantine vocabulary and mark each existing path as write-closed, migration-candidate, or actively governed.

description: Repository-health outputs are partly routed to the desired maintenance areas but many still land at the root or in ad hoc folders.
evidence: Root contains cleanup and audit markdown/csv files while reports/maintenance already exists and tables/ contains maintenance-friendly csv space.
impact: Governance work itself contributes to the clutter it is trying to reduce.
recommended_resolution: Route all future maintenance reports to reports/maintenance and maintenance tables to named tables/maintenance_*.csv outputs.

affected_paths: docs; reports; surveys; canonical; root; module docs folders
description: Durable human-readable documentation exists in several unrelated namespaces.
evidence: Markdown is present at the root, in docs/, reports/, surveys/, canonical/, Aging/, Relaxation ver3/, Switching/, and other source folders.
impact: Readers have no single discovery path for stable architecture, policy, and scientific narrative documents.
recommended_resolution: Use docs/ for policies and architecture, reports/<module>/ for durable study outputs, and keep module-local docs only when tightly coupled to source internals.

description: Run folders include m-files, which may be valuable lineage snapshots, but the repository does not clearly declare whether those copies are immutable evidence or live source.
evidence: Representative run directories contain one m-file plus csv/txt/json sidecars.
impact: Users may edit or treat run snapshots as source code, risking lineage confusion.
recommended_resolution: Document run-folder source snapshots as immutable execution lineage copies or formalize a separate snapshot subfolder name.

description: Third-party color assets and helper code live in a first-party sounding top-level directory.
evidence: github_repo contains cmocean and ScientificColourMaps8 with many palettes and asset formats.
impact: Ownership and cleanup expectations are unclear compared with first-party scientific code.
recommended_resolution: Document this area as vendored external content now; consider a future vendor/ namespace only after license and code-reference checks.
